kept.text:
Observed activity suggests that two previously identified market-making entities have resumed coordinated behavior, directing artificial off-chain trading volume for $DOG (Bitcoin) toward Bitget and Gate, with reduced activity on Kraken.

A review of on-chain flows indicates a tightly interconnected network of wallets exhibiting synchronized inflows and outflows, reinforcing the likelihood of deliberate structuring.

Notably, long-term holder concentration remains elevated at 79.8%, implying that recent price movements may not reflect organic distribution. Instead, the divergence between stable holder behavior and volatile price action raises the possibility of off-chain mechanisms influencing market perception, potentially prompting retail participants to exit positions that are subsequently accumulated and redistributed across intermediary wallets.

On-chain data continues to provide a more transparent and verifiable account of market dynamics than off-chain activity.

The “Sell in May and go away” narrative may be more relevant to Bitcoin than many assume.

Historical drawdowns around this period:

🔴 2014: −60%
🔴 2018: −57%
🔴 2022: −67%

Each cycle saw sharp corrections emerge around May, often marking the start of a deeper downside.

Is this going to repeat this cycle too? 😳

kept.text:
GM, and Happy Sunday

quick one for the timeline today, the Bitcoin quantum debate has 3 camps right now

> BIP-361 wants to phase out vulnerable addresses and freeze 1.1M BTC including Satoshi's coins

> Sztorc wants to hard fork the entire chain into 7 sidechains

> @quipnetwork just shipped a third option that needs no fork, no consensus change, no community vote

WOTS+ signatures layered on ArchNtwrk smart contracts. anchored directly to Bitcoin. narrows the quantum attack window to about 20 minutes.

6.9M BTC currently sitting in quantum vulnerable addresses including Satoshi's stash. 

the other proposals argue about which holders to confiscate from. this one just gives users an opt in.

Bitcoin devs say a protocol upgrade could take 5 to 10 years. Quip's approach delivers similar protection now.

while the rest argue about whose coins to freeze, some people are already protecting theirs

$QUIP

quoted_tweet.text:
I’m honestly shocked at how many are calling the bottom of bitcoin here. 

Whats even more interesting is the amount of people that are angry at the idea it could go lower. 

Here’s the cycles data:

Daily cycle bottomed last week but this is the last daily cycle in the weekly cycle. So nothing more than a counter trend bounce is expected before heading down into a weekly cycle low at the end of May. 

Then we have the first real chance at a bottom. But on a balance of probabilities, we get something like 2-3 months of counter trend bounce / chopsolidation, followed by another decline into late 26 for a weekly and 4 year cycle low. 

So the way to stack the probabilities in your favour is to wait for late May to hunt for leadership and then be open to adding more late 26. 

The absolute best case scenario price wise is we range sideways for the rest of the year. But that would be something we almost never see for 4 year cycle lows. I actually can’t even think of a 4 year (or any other major yearly) cycle low that formed via time (if anyone has one please let me know) 

So people betting on the lows being in here don’t realise they’re betting on a 10% probability outcome.

kept.text:
The problem with candlestick analysis, if you take even one moment to think about it is that it tells you what happened in the past. It doesn't tell you what's going to happen in the future. 

Take for instance right now. We have what can be defined as a bullish engulfing. All the red has been taken over by green. Great so does that mean we're going to keep going up? Well, you have to wait for the bullish engulfing to close. So do you wait for the 1-hour candle? 4 hour? Daily? 3-day? Weekly? Monthly? Quarterly? If you really want confirmation you would wait for the quarterly candle to close. That means you can't actually make any trades for 4 months. 4 months later if the price is doubled, you got your confirmation and you got your target filled. The only thing you didn't get is profit.

kept.text:
gQUIP

Bitcoin’s quantum threat is real, but @QuipNetwork just introduced a new path forward today.

For years, the debate has been stuck: freeze vulnerable coins, push a hard fork, or wait through slow consensus cycles. Meanwhile, the risks tied to ECDSA remain part of Bitcoin’s current design. Even Satoshi Nakamoto acknowledged long-term cryptographic risks.

$Quip’s approach is different. No base layer changes. No governance battles. No disruption to existing holders.

By layering WOTS+ on top of Bitcoin, it introduces post-quantum protection without touching the protocol itself.

What that means in practice:

• Additive security on top of existing Bitcoin signatures
• Attack window reduced to roughly two blocks, around 20 minutes
• No need to move coins or migrate wallets
• Open-source implementation
• Consumer wallet expected to launch soon

Instead of waiting years for protocol-level upgrades, this is a live attempt to extend Bitcoin’s security model externally.

The bigger question isn’t whether quantum risk exists.
It’s how early you act on it.

Who’s already thinking about securing their stack?

profile_bio.description: Crypto addict || community Builder || Moderator || Crypto Trader || Space Host || Knowledge MAXI 📕 📚 |

kept.text:
In 2013, a programmer named James Howells threw away a laptop.

He was cleaning his house in Newport, Wales. The laptop was old. He didn't think about what was on it.

What was on it: a hard drive containing 8,000 Bitcoin he had mined in 2009.

He realized the mistake about a week later.

The laptop was already in a landfill. A specific landfill, covering 350 acres, receiving 80,000 tons of waste per year.

He contacted the Newport City Council and asked permission to dig.

They said no. Environmental regulations. Groundwater risk. Cost.

He offered them 25% of whatever he found. That was $182 million at the time.

They still said no.

He has been fighting them ever since. For over a decade. Lawyers, engineers, environmental impact reports.

As of 2024, Bitcoin has risen further. His coins are worth over $700 million.

They are somewhere under that field in Wales.

The council still says no.

Every time Bitcoin goes up, his landfill gets more expensive.

He checks the price every day.

kept.text:
👀 Un homme a eut une idée ingénieuse en utilisant Bitcoin.

Un court message, intégré dans la blockchain, qui ne se détériore pas, ne peut pas être modifié et ne peut pas être retiré.

Autour de cela, il a construit un certificat physique comme ancre dans le monde réel.
Papier de haute qualité, papier doré, embossage, design épuré, plus un code QR liant à la transaction.

L'objectif est que cela ressemble à quelque chose de significatif que les gens conserveront ou offriront.

Vous en pensez quoi ?

$BTC ビットコインは4月の急騰後にさらなる下落に直面する可能性？

CryptoQuantはビットコインの現在の需要パターンが2022年の弱気市場の始まりを反映していると述べています。当時、先物需要が急増する一方で現物需要が減少していました。

ビットコインの4月の20%上昇は主に永久先物トレーダーによるもので強い現物買いによるものではありませんでした。

CryptoQuantのチャートは、このような状況が「最終的に持続的な価格下落を先取りした」ことを示しています。

kept.text:
Think Bitcoin is just hype? Think again.

In 2009, launched Bitcoin quietly.
At the start, it had no real market price.

By 2010, it traded around $0.003.
People ignored it, some even got BTC for free.

Then came the first real use, 10,000 BTC for pizza.
That moment gave Bitcoin real-world value.

From cents to $1, then crossed $1000 in 2013. Each cycle brought more attention and belief.

In 2021, it reached around $69,000.
Now it trades in tens of thousands of dollars.

From almost zero to global asset.
Bitcoin proved small ideas can change the world.
